canto_title: Beatrice's Theological Discourse on the Incarnation and Redemption

scenes.summary: Beatrice sings a hymn of praise to the Holy God.
The celestial substances and lights dance and move with great speed.
Dante feels overwhelmed by a sense of reverence and hesitation.
Beatrice smiles and promises to resolve Dante's mental confusion.
Beatrice explains how the sin of Adam caused humanity to deviate from the path of truth.
She describes the Crucifixion as a just response to the injury of nature.
Dante expresses his difficulty in understanding the necessity of this divine method.
Beatrice introduces the idea of a divine decree that is hidden from those without love.
She explains how divine goodness radiates and illuminates all things.
She describes how sin makes the human creature dissimilar to the supreme good.

scenes.scene_name: The Necessity of a Way Back
scenes.summary: Beatrice explains that humanity could only be restored through divine grace or a divine gift.
She asserts that man could not have redeemed himself through mere obedience or humility.
She discusses why God chose the path of the Incarnation to restore humanity.
Beatrice highlights the greatness of the Son of God descending to earth.
Beatrice shifts Dante's attention back to his visual perception.
Dante observes the corruption and fleeting nature of the material elements.
Beatrice explains how angels, elements, and celestial stars are formed and animated.
She describes how the human soul is uniquely infused with divine goodness.
Beatrice concludes with a logical point regarding the future resurrection of the flesh.
